```{r}
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

pip_tbl <- tibble::tribble(
  ~outcome, ~exposure,  ~PIP,
  "ASI_p",  "Ba_air",   0.8824,
  "ASI_p",  "Se_air",   0.7756,
  "ASI_p",  "Cu_air",   0.7580,
  "ASI_p",  "Mn_air",   0.7432,
  "ASI_p",  "Mo_air",   0.7372,
  "ASI_p",  "V_air",    0.7168,
  "ASI_p",  "S_air",    0.6844,
  "ASI_p",  "Co_air",   0.6796,
  "ASI_p",  "La_air",   0.6736,
  "ASI_p",  "Cd_air",   0.6724,
  "PCL",    "Ba_air",   0.5740,
  "ASI_s",  "Cd_air",   0.5352,
  "PCL",    "Cd_air",   0.5156,
  "ASI_p",  "Co_wipe",  0.9764,
  "ASI_p",  "Sr_wipe",  0.9756,
  "ASI_p",  "V_wipe",   0.9544,
  "ASI_p",  "Cd_wipe",  0.9468,
  "ASI_p",  "Mn_wipe",  0.8540,
  "ASI_p",  "Cr_wipe",  0.8488,
  "ASI_p",  "Ba_wipe",  0.8248,
  "ASI_p",  "Mo_wipe",  0.8104,
  "ASI_p",  "Cu_wipe",  0.7280,
  "ASI_p",  "Ag_wipe",  0.5688
)
plot_df <- pip_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    medium = case_when(
      str_ends(exposure, "_air")  ~ "air",
      str_ends(exposure, "_wipe") ~ "wipe",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    toxicant = str_remove(exposure, "_(air|wipe)$")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(medium)) %>%
  mutate(
    medium  = factor(medium, levels = c("air", "wipe")),
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("ASI_p", "ASI_s", "PCL"))
  )


tox_group <- plot_df %>%
  distinct(toxicant, medium) %>%
  group_by(toxicant) %>%
  summarise(
    has_air  = any(medium == "air"),
    has_wipe = any(medium == "wipe"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    has_air & has_wipe ~ "overlap",
    has_wipe           ~ "wipe_only",
    has_air            ~ "air_only",
    TRUE               ~ "other"
  ))

tox_rank <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(toxicant) %>%
  summarise(maxP = max(PIP, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(tox_group, by = "toxicant") %>%
  mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("overlap", "wipe_only", "air_only"))) %>%
  arrange(group, desc(maxP))

tox_levels <- tox_rank$toxicant

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(toxicant = factor(toxicant, levels = tox_levels))

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = toxicant, y = medium)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = PIP, color = outcome), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("ASI_p" = "orange", "ASI_s" = "lightblue", "PCL" = "purple")) +
  labs(x = "Toxicants", y = NULL, size = "PIP", color = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 13),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 6)),
    size = guide_legend()
  )
```


# Univariate Exposure-Response analysis
## tox_wipe_covar
PART 1: Compute and store BKMR univariate ER curves
```{r}
# --- PART 1: Compute and store BKMR univariate ER curves ---
univar_ER_wipe <- list()

for (dep in mh_domains) {
  cat("Calculating BKMR univariate ER curves for:", dep, "...\n")
  
  # 1. outcome
  Y <- tox_wipe_covar[[dep]]
  
  # 2. exposure matrix (PCA-selected wipe metals)
  Z <- tox_wipe_covar %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(pca_selected_metals_wipe)) %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # 3. covariates
  selected_covars <- lasso_selected_vars_wipe[[dep]]
  if (length(selected_covars) <= 1) {
    X <- NULL
  } else {
    X <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = tox_wipe_covar[, selected_covars, drop = FALSE])
  }
  
  # 4. Fit BKMR
  set.seed(303)
  fit_km <- kmbayes(
    y      = Y,
    Z      = Z,
    X      = X,
    iter   = 5000,
    varsel = TRUE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  
  # 5. Compute univariate exposure-response curves
  er_curves <- PredictorResponseUnivar(
    fit     = fit_km,
    sel     = 1:ncol(Z),
    method  = "approx"
  )
  
  # Save result for this outcome
  univar_ER_wipe[[dep]] <- er_curves
  
  cat("  >> Finished", dep, "\n\n")
}
```


part 2: Plot ER curves for one mental health domain
```{r}
# ---- Plot ER curves for 7 mental health domains ----

dep_to_plot <- "ASI"
er_obj <- univar_ER_wipe[[dep_to_plot]]
metal_names <- pca_selected_metals_wipe

# Constant used in log10(x + const)
shift_const <- 0.0001428715

for (j in seq_along(metal_names)) {
  
  metal_name <- metal_names[j]
  
  df_plot <- er_obj %>% 
    filter(variable == metal_name)
  
  if (nrow(df_plot) < 2) {
    message("No ER curve for ", metal_name)
    next
  }
  
  df_plot$upper <- df_plot$est + 1.96 * df_plot$se
  df_plot$lower <- df_plot$est - 1.96 * df_plot$se

  # Compute tick marks in log-scale and convert to raw scale
  xticks_log <- pretty(df_plot$z)
  xticks_raw <- round(10^xticks_log - shift_const, 3)

  plot(df_plot$z, df_plot$est, type = "l", lwd = 2,
       xlab = paste0(metal_name, " (ppb)"),
       ylab = paste("Estimated ER for", dep_to_plot),
       main = paste("BKMR Univariate ER Curve:", metal_name),
       xaxt = "n")
  
  axis(side = 1, at = xticks_log, labels = xticks_raw)
  
  lines(df_plot$z, df_plot$upper, lty = 2, col = "gray40")
  lines(df_plot$z, df_plot$lower, lty = 2, col = "gray40")
}


```

part 3: all in one plot
```{r}
# Dependent variables
dep_vars <- c("CESD", "ASI", "ASI_p", "ASI_c", "ASI_s", "PCL", "FAST")

# Constant used in log10(x + const)
shift_const <- 0.0001428715

for (dep in dep_vars) {
  
  cat("Plotting ER curves for:", dep, "\n")
  
  er_obj <- univar_ER_wipe[[dep]]
  
  df_all <- er_obj
  
  # Convert log10(x + c) → raw x value (ppb) for label display
  df_all <- df_all %>%
    mutate(
      z_raw = round(10^z - shift_const, 3)
    )

  p <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = z, y = est)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(
      title = paste("BKMR Univariate Exposure–Response Curves for", dep),
      x = "Exposure (ppb)",  # <-- changed to ppb
      y = paste("Estimated ER for", dep)
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = pretty(df_all$z),
      labels = function(z) round(10^z - shift_const, 3)
    )
  
  print(p)
  
 # Save each plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("ER_curve_wipe_", dep, ".png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
}


```

## tox_air_covar
PART 1: Compute and store BKMR univariate ER curves
```{r}
# --- PART 1: Compute and store BKMR univariate ER curves ---
univar_ER_air <- list()

for (dep in mh_domains) {
  cat("Calculating BKMR univariate ER curves for:", dep, "...\n")
  
  # 1. outcome
  Y <- tox_air_covar[[dep]]
  
  # 2. exposure matrix (PCA-selected air metals)
  Z <- tox_air_covar %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(pca_selected_metals_air)) %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # 3. covariates
  selected_covars <- lasso_selected_vars_air[[dep]]
  if (length(selected_covars) <= 1) {
    X <- NULL
  } else {
    X <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = tox_air_covar[, selected_covars, drop = FALSE])
  }
  
  # 4. Fit BKMR
  set.seed(303)
  fit_km <- kmbayes(
    y      = Y,
    Z      = Z,
    X      = X,
    iter   = 5000,
    varsel = TRUE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  
  # 5. Compute univariate exposure-response curves
  er_curves <- PredictorResponseUnivar(
    fit     = fit_km,
    sel     = 1:ncol(Z),
    method  = "approx"
  )
  
  # Save result for this outcome
  univar_ER_air[[dep]] <- er_curves
  
  cat("  >> Finished", dep, "\n\n")
}
```

part 2: Plot ER curves for one mental health domain
```{r}
# ---- Plot ER curves for 7 mental health domains ----

dep_to_plot <- "ASI_p"
er_obj <- univar_ER_air[[dep_to_plot]]
metal_names <- pca_selected_metals_air

# Constant used in log10(x + const)
shift_const <- 0.0001428715

for (j in seq_along(metal_names)) {
  
  metal_name <- metal_names[j]
  
  df_plot <- er_obj %>% 
    filter(variable == metal_name)
  
  if (nrow(df_plot) < 2) {
    message("No ER curve for ", metal_name)
    next
  }
  
  df_plot$upper <- df_plot$est + 1.96 * df_plot$se
  df_plot$lower <- df_plot$est - 1.96 * df_plot$se

  # Compute tick marks in log-scale and convert to raw scale
  xticks_log <- pretty(df_plot$z)
  xticks_raw <- round(10^xticks_log - shift_const, 3)

  plot(df_plot$z, df_plot$est, type = "l", lwd = 2,
       xlab = bquote(.(metal_name) ~ "(" * mu * "g/" * m^3 * ")"),
       ylab = paste("Estimated ER for", dep_to_plot),
       main = paste("BKMR Univariate ER Curve:", metal_name),
       xaxt = "n")
  
  axis(side = 1, at = xticks_log, labels = xticks_raw)
  
  lines(df_plot$z, df_plot$upper, lty = 2, col = "gray40")
  lines(df_plot$z, df_plot$lower, lty = 2, col = "gray40")
}


```

part 3: all in one plot
```{r}
# Dependent variables
dep_vars <- c("CESD", "ASI", "ASI_p", "ASI_c", "ASI_s", "PCL", "FAST")

# Constant used in log10(x + const)
shift_const <- 0.0001428715

for (dep in dep_vars) {
  
  cat("Plotting ER curves for:", dep, "\n")
  
  er_obj <- univar_ER_air[[dep]]
  
  df_all <- er_obj
  
  # Convert log10(x + c) → raw x value (ppb) for label display
  df_all <- df_all %>%
    mutate(
      z_raw = round(10^z - shift_const, 3)
    )

  p <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = z, y = est)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(
      title = paste("BKMR Univariate Exposure–Response Curves for", dep),
      x = expression("Exposure (" * mu * "g/" * m^3 * ")"),
      y = paste("Estimated ER for", dep)
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = pretty(df_all$z),
      labels = function(z) round(10^z - shift_const, 3)
    )
  
  print(p)
  
  # Save each plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("ER_curve_air_", dep, ".png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
}


```
